text: Fill in the blanks about the pons.
questions: The pons is divided into a ventral part called the *basis pontis* and a dorsal part called the *tegmentum/pontine tegmentum*.
The *facial/CN VII* nerve fibres loop over the *abducens/CN VI* nucleus to form the genu of CN VII, creating the facial *colliculus* on the 4th ventricle floor.
In locked-in syndrome caused by pontine haemorrhage, the patient is fully *conscious/aware* but can only communicate through *vertical/vertical eye* movements.
The *middle cerebellar peduncle/middle* is the largest peduncle and carries *pontocerebellar/corticopontocerebellar* fibres from the pontine nuclei to the cerebellum.
